project_manifest.name: Universal Office
project_manifest.operational_status: OPERATIONAL — v2.7.0: Phase 6.6 Audit Remediation complete. Ethiopian Third Hour ID mismatch resolved. Markdown documentation backlog fully resolved.

critical_bugs: Ethiopian ID mismatch: 'eth-meserk-hour-text' in components/ethiopian.json vs expected 'eth-meserkh-hour-text' in rubrics.
Rubric logic: [rite] placeholder resolution in rubrics.json is fragile and prone to null returns.
Tooltip collision: js/tooltip.js lacks vertical overflow detection for elements at the bottom of the viewport.
architectural_debt: Heavy reliance on .innerHTML string building in office-ui.js needs transition to a template system.
Global state race condition: appData hydration must be gated before renderOffice() execution.
Redundant liturgical fragments (Gloria Patri/Kyrie) should be normalized into common.json.

resolved_audit_items.item: CalendarEngine: Missing Gregorian-to-Ethiopian date conversion for Senkessar resolution.
resolved_audit_items.resolution: Resolved in Phase 7.4 via full Julian Day Number algorithm implementation.

changes: Added third mode button: 'The Ethiopian Sa'atat (Book of Hours)' with class ethiopian-btn, calling selectMode('ethiopian-saatat')

rubric_loop_handlers: VARIABLE_OPENING — resolves bcp-opening-${season}, fallback to bcp-opening-general
VARIABLE_ANTIPHON — resolves antiphon_mp or antiphon_ep from dailyData
VARIABLE_PSALM — renders appointed psalms with optional Gloria Patri
VARIABLE_READING_OT — OT lesson from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_EPISTLE — Epistle from independent morning or evening chain
VARIABLE_READING_GOSPEL — Gospel subject to gospelPlacement setting
VARIABLE_COLLECT — collect lookup with manual ID mappings; triggers Examen and Kyrie Pantocrator
VARIABLE_WEEKDAY_COLLECT — tiered fallback: dailyData.collect_weekday → bcp-collect-grace/peace → skip

VARIABLE_READING_ET — canonical ET-tradition scripture reading; resolves ethHourInfo.etReading; active for Nigatu (1 Clement) and both Le'lit watches (Shepherd of Hermas); silently skipped for all other hours
eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) renders rubric_before as italic rubric label, then full Tselote Meweta text (Lord's Prayer + Thanksgiving + Psalm 50/51). Logs warning if component missing.
eth-saatat-hour-slot — resolves canonical hour text from getEthiopianHourInfo().hourId; injects italicised Sixth Hour commemorative sentence when hourId === 'eth-lika-hour-text'
eth-mazmur-slot — renders appointed Psalms via ethHourInfo.psalms; closes with eth-anqasa-birhan. Full edition prepends 12-fold Leke Haile chant.

eth-saints-commemoration — FULL SENKESSAR PIPELINE: calls EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate(), lazy-loads and caches senkessar-index.json, fetches and caches per-day narrative from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{monthSlug}/{day}.json. Falls back to Oriental saints filter, then generic intercession. MONTH_SLUG_MAP handles miyazya→miazia.
key_logic_notes: Reading resolution uses independent morning/evening chains — no shared intermediate variables between MP and EP
Theotokion uses two-step lookup: cop-theotokion-${season} with fallback to cop-theotokion

Examen text: \n\n converted to

, rendered in
toggleSidebar() detects active panel by checking which is NOT mode-hidden — works for both BCP and Ethiopian panels
CalendarEngine.init() called explicitly in init() after JSON loads, before fetchLectionaryData()
resolvedOfficeId = window._forcedOfficeId || officeId — used throughout renderOffice() to support ethiopian-saatat override
Saints data preloaded before sequence loop so eth-saints-commemoration has access on first render
isEthiopianSaatat flag gates all BCP-specific pre/post-sequence blocks
getEthiopianHourInfo() returns exhaustive 9-entry 24-hour map covering all canonical watches
window._temporalOverride — date and hourId override for testing any canonical hour at any date
MONTH_SLUG_MAP in eth-saints-commemoration normalises EthiopianCalendar month names to folder slugs; handles miyazya→miazia and teqemt→tiqimt irregularities
Senkessar index and per-day narratives cached in appData.senkessarIndex and appData.senkessarCache — no redundant fetches

js/calendar-engine.js.role: All date and liturgical calendar logic
public_methods: init() — loads bcp-propers.json; must be called before fetchLectionaryData()
getSeasonAndFile(date) — returns { season, file, liturgicalColor, litYear }
fetchLectionaryData(date) — returns daily entry object from seasonal JSON

js/calendar-ethiopian.js.role: Ethiopian (Ge'ez) calendar conversion and date resolution
js/calendar-ethiopian.js.status: COMPLETE AND OPERATIONAL — Phase 7.4. Fully integrated.
public_methods: getEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns { day, month, monthIndex, year } in Amete Mihret era
getCopticDate(gregorianDate) — same algorithm, Coptic month names, EC-276 era
formatEthiopianDate(gregorianDate) — returns display string e.g. '15 Yekatit 2018'
MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Ge'ez month name strings
COPTIC_MONTH_NAMES — exported array of 13 Coptic month name strings
implementation_notes: Julian Day Number (JDN) method — valid across all historical and future dates
Epoch: 1 Meskerem 1 EC = JDN 1724221 = 29 August 8 AD (Julian)
Pagume: 5 days normally, 6 in leap years (year % 4 === 3)
Self-test suite on load — 9 verified reference points logged to console
js/calendar-ethiopian.js.integration_status: COMPLETE. Previously listed as 'Phase 8.2 target — not yet wired'. That was incorrect or stale. EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate() is actively called in the eth-saints-commemoration handler and drives all Senkessar date resolution. formatEthiopianDate() is also available for Phase 8.5 Ge'ez date display — no additional engine work needed for that feature.

js/scripture-resolver.js.role: Scripture text fetching, citation parsing, LRU book cache

css/office.css.role: All application styles — theming, sidebar, layout, print
changes: Added .ethiopian-theme .rubric-text — Metsehafe Tselot aesthetic: #C0392B, 0.85em, weight 300, italic, IM Fell English, font-variant normal, border-left 2px solid #C0392B, padding-left 10px, border-bottom none, margin-top 28px, text-shadow none, width auto [v2.6.1]
Added .ethiopian-theme .component-text — font-weight 500 for hierarchy of voice [v2.6.1]
Added @media print override — collapses Ethiopian rubric border-left and padding [v2.6.1]
Color note: #C0392B used for dark mode legibility. body.light-mode override restores #8B0000 (traditional Qay ink) for manuscript mode [v2.7.1].
key_rules: #settings-panel — BCP sidebar, transition: transform 0.3s ease, translateX(-336px) when hidden
#main-content — flex content area, padding-left 80px when sidebar hidden
.ethiopian-btn — gold-bordered splash button for Sa'atat entry

js/css/office.css.note: Was never referenced by index.html or any JS module. Deleted to eliminate confusion.

ethiopian_tradition.status: OPERATIONAL — v2.6.1 complete. Full 24-hour canonical cycle, Tselote Meweta opening, Senkessar wired, Metsehafe Tselot rubric aesthetic.
ethiopian_tradition.entry_point: selectMode('ethiopian-saatat') — sets window._forcedOfficeId, hides BCP sidebar, shows ethiopian-settings, applies .ethiopian-theme to body
ethiopian_tradition.rubrics_load: Loaded in init() and concatenated onto appData.rubrics after data/rubrics.json
components: eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) Tselote Meweta mandatory opening: prostration rubric, Lord's Prayer, Thanksgiving, Psalm 50/51 with Gloria Patri
eth-basalios-prayer — St. Basil's foundational prayer (all hours)
eth-prayer-thanksgiving — RETIRED FROM SEQUENCE v2.6.1. Content subsumed by eth-introduction-to-every-hour. Retained in JSON as reference.
eth-anqasa-birhan — Gate of Light Theotokos hymn; appended after Mazmur
eth-lika-hour-text — Sixth Hour / Lika (12:00–15:00). Crucifixion hour. ID is 'lika' not 'liku' — corrected in rubrics metadata v2.6.1.
eth-mahlet-hour-text — Pre-dawn Vigil / Mahlet (03:00–06:00). NOTE: text flagged as sparse/provisional in component note — full text should be sourced from qualified scholar.
eth-leke-haile-chant — 12-fold Leke Haile doxology (Full edition only)
eth-41-kyrie — 41-fold Kyrie / Igzee'o Tesahalene (Full edition only)
eth-saints-commemoration — Senkessar pipeline component + Oriental saints fallback + intercession fallback
eth-synaxarium-reading — Senkessar reading scaffold; actual pipeline implemented within eth-saints-commemoration handler
eth-weddase-sunday through eth-weddase-saturday — Weddase Maryam for all seven days
sequence_current: eth-saatat-hour-slot            ← active canonical hour + Sixth Hour sentence
eth-mazmur-slot                 ← Psalms + Anqasa Birhan (+ Leke Haile in Full)

12:00–15:00.note: Crucifixion hour — commemorative sentence injected in renderer

ethiopian_tradition.saints_filter: Only saints where tradition includes 'ethiopian' or 'oriental' (case-insensitive). Used only when no Senkessar entry found. Final fallback: generic Oriental Orthodox Communion intercession.
ethiopian_tradition.bcp_isolation: All BCP-specific blocks gated behind !isEthiopianSaatat throughout renderOffice()

ethiopian_synaxarium.status: OPERATIONAL — Fully wired and rendering. Index validated. 12 of 13 months confirmed complete. Hamle status requires developer verification.
ethiopian_synaxarium.description: Senkessar (Ethiopian Synaxarium) integrated in eth-saints-commemoration rubric handler. Renders for all hours via EthiopianCalendar date resolution and dynamic fetch from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{month}/{day}.json.
ethiopian_synaxarium.wiring_status: COMPLETE. Previously listed as 'Phase 8.2 target — not yet wired'. This was stale. EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate() is called directly in eth-saints-commemoration. Senkessar index lazy-loaded and cached. Per-day narratives fetched and cached.
ethiopian_synaxarium.index_status: COMPLETE — 13 months, 385 entries. Corrected and validated 2026-02-23.
index_schema_notes: months is a top-level JSON array (not object)
ge'ez key renamed to ge_ez for parser compatibility
All months use uniform days[] array (not entries{} object)
Each day: { day, id, title } — id pattern: senkessar-{month-slug}-{day}
Months 6–10 converted from entries{} to days[] during 2026-02-23 correction

miazia_path_resolution.issue: EthiopianCalendar outputs 'Miyazya'. Folder on disk is 'miazia'.
miazia_path_resolution.status: RESOLVED IN RENDERER — MONTH_SLUG_MAP maps 'miyazya': 'miazia'. No folder rename or index change required for correct operation.
miazia_path_resolution.optional_cleanup: Cosmetic only: consider aligning senkessar-index.json month 8 slug to 'miazia' in a future maintenance pass.

meskerem.note: Enkutatash New Year, Maskal (Finding of True Cross) arc

tiqimt.note: Martyrs of Najran, interior life arc

hidar.note: Arba'etu Insesa, Entry of Virgin into Temple, Archangel Michael Great Feast arc

tahsas.note: Incarnation arc — Annunciation through Nativity (Ganna) through Innocents

tir.note: Timqat arc. Circumcision (Day 6), Eve of Timqat (Day 10), Timqat/Epiphany (Day 11), Cana (Day 13), Anthony the Great (Day 17), Three Hierarchs (Day 30). Generated 2026-02-23.

yekatit.note: Kidane Meheret (Covenant of Mercy, Day 16) arc

megabit.note: Finding of Cross (Day 10), Annunciation/Tinsae Maryam (Day 29) arc

miazia.note: Folder slug confirmed 'miazia'. Renderer MONTH_SLUG_MAP correct. George the Great Martyr (Day 23), Mark the Evangelist (Day 25) arc.

ginbot.note: 318 Holy Fathers of Nicaea (Day 10), Pachomius (Day 14), Constantine & Helena (Day 21) arc

sene.note: Nativity of John the Baptist (Day 24), Peter and Paul (Day 30) arc

hamle.note: Verified 2026-02-24. 30 narrative JSON files confirmed present on disk. Key feast: Haile Maryam / Blessed Virgin Mary (Day 21).

pagumen.note: Intercalary month (5 days; 6 in leap years). Archangel Raphael (Day 3) arc.

narrative_format.narrative_style: Deep Assembly — High-liturgical English; 4-paragraph woven structure: (1) primary monthly commemoration in theological arc, (2) Discovery saints woven by name etymology, (3) historical/patristic anchor, (4) theological seal to month apex feast
narrative_format.register: Formal high-liturgical English; no modern idioms; paragraph breaks as \n\n

tir_narrative_arc.apex_feast: 11 Tir — Timqat (Epiphany) — Trinitarian theophany, hallowing of all waters
tir_narrative_arc.approach_days_1_10: Adam, Job, Zechariah, John Apostle, Philip, Circumcision of Christ (Day 6), Gabriel, Mary, Samuel, Gera/Eve of Timqat (Day 10)
tir_narrative_arc.apex_day_11: Timqat — Son in water, Spirit as dove, Father from opened heaven; reversal of Eden; Michael; Theodosius Cenobiarch
tir_narrative_arc.post_epiphanic_days_12_20: Arba'etu Insesa (Day 12), Cana (Day 13), Paul the Hermit (Day 15), Anthony the Great (Day 17), Macarius (Day 19), Euthymius (Day 20)
tir_narrative_arc.synthesis_days_21_30: Mary (Day 21), Maximus Confessor, patriarchs (Day 28), Ephrem the Syrian (Day 28), Triple Feast (Day 29), Three Hierarchs — Basil/Gregory/Chrysostom (Day 30)

data_summary.note: Individual data files not enumerated here. See historical structure file for full listings.
data_summary.data/bible/: [600+ Scripture files across NT, OT, ET, SY, AR, ODES subdirectories]
data_summary.data/season/: [9 seasonal JSON files — 353 total BCP 1979 daily lectionary entries, Year One and Year Two]
data_summary.data/components.json: DEPRECATED — do not use. Stale pre-modularization monolith.
data_summary.data/synaxarium/ethiopian/tir/: 30 narrative JSON files — complete Tir corpus (Phase 8.1)
data_summary.data/synaxarium/ethiopian/hamle/: 30 narrative JSON files — complete Hamle corpus. Verified 2026-02-24.
data_summary.data/synaxarium/ethiopian/[all months]/: Complete narrative corpora confirmed for all 13 months: meskerem, tiqimt, hidar, tahsas, tir, yekatit, megabit, miazia, ginbot, sene, hamle, nehase, pagumen.
data_summary.components/ethiopian.json: ~19 Ethiopian Tewahedo components (v2.6.1: +eth-introduction-to-every-hour; eth-prayer-thanksgiving retained but retired from sequence)
Ethiopian Sa'atat rubric. v2.6.1: eth-introduction-to-every-hour prepended; eth-prayer-thanksgiving removed; eth-liku typo corrected to eth-lika. v2.7.0: eth-meserkh-hour-text typo corrected to eth-meserk-hour-text in slots time_map.

eth_mahlet_text.description: eth-mahlet-hour-text (03:00–06:00) component note flags text as sparse/provisional. Drawn from SAATAT_FULL ch.1.
eth_mahlet_text.action: Full Mahlet text should be sourced from a qualified Ethiopian Orthodox liturgical scholar. Not blocking.

recent_logic_changes.change: Phase 6.6 Audit Remediation — Third Hour ID mismatch corrected; structure.json updated
recent_logic_changes.detail: eth-meserkh-hour-text corrected to eth-meserk-hour-text in rubrics.json slots time_map. structure.json: documentation_debt object deleted (backlog fully resolved); CalendarEngine Senkessar resolution bug moved to resolved_audit_items with Phase 7.4 resolution note; next_required updated; version bumped to 2.7.0.

recent_logic_changes.change: Internal code audit identifying rendering race conditions, tooltip collision bugs, and Ethiopian ID mismatches.
recent_logic_changes.detail: Identified innerHTML technical debt, appData hydration race conditions, tooltip y-axis overflow, and eth-meserk-hour-text ID typo.
Mandatory introductory component for all canonical hours. rubric_before field (punchy prostration instruction), Lord's Prayer, hour-generic Thanksgiving, full Psalm 50/51 with Gloria Patri.
Content subsumed by eth-introduction-to-every-hour. Removed from rubrics.json sequence. Component retained in JSON.

recent_logic_changes.change: eth-introduction-to-every-hour prepended to sequence; eth-prayer-thanksgiving removed; eth-liku→eth-lika corrected in slots metadata
recent_logic_changes.detail: Sequence now opens with Tselote Meweta. ID typo was cosmetic — renderer used JS hourMap, not rubrics slots, for resolution.

recent_logic_changes.change: Added eth-introduction-to-every-hour handler; added Sixth Hour commemorative sentence in eth-saatat-hour-slot
recent_logic_changes.detail: Intro handler renders rubric_before then full text. Sixth Hour: 'The Sixth Hour — the hour of the Crucifixion. Bow your head.' injected when hourId === 'eth-lika-hour-text'.

recent_logic_changes.change: Ethiopian rubric Metsehafe Tselot aesthetic; color corrected #8B0000→#C0392B; print override
recent_logic_changes.detail: #8B0000 illegible on dark parchment. #C0392B matches --rubric dark mode variable and is legible. Print collapses border-left.

recent_logic_changes.change: DELETED — orphaned duplicate. Never referenced by any file.

roadmap_summary.completed: Phases 6.1–6.5, Phase 6.6 (Audit Remediation — Third Hour ID mismatch corrected, documentation backlog fully resolved), Phase 7.1–7.4 (full Ethiopian Sa'atat + calendar engine), Phase 8.1 (Senkessar index + Tir corpus + full wiring via EthiopianCalendar), Phase 8.3 (Hamle narrative corpus — verified complete 2026-02-24), v2.6.1 (Tselote Meweta, Metsehafe Tselot aesthetic, fixes)
roadmap_summary.next_required: Phase 6.6 Audit Remediation items remaining: tooltip vertical overflow detection (js/tooltip.js) and appData hydration race condition (js/office-ui.js). Markdown documentation backlog has been completely resolved.
roadmap_summary.next_planned: Phase 8.5 — Ge'ez date display in Sa'atat UI header. EthiopianCalendar.formatEthiopianDate() already available — UI change only, no engine work needed.
roadmap_summary.future: Phase 6.7 — Performance polish (timezone warning, LRU seasonal cache, prefetch, service worker); Phase 7.5 — Coptic Agpeya as standalone tradition entry; Phase 9.0 — Mobile app; Phase 9.1 — Cross-Tradition Search (Comparative scripture view across AR, ET, and SY canons)
